cinema.name: Cinema E
cinema.description: Welcome to the Hermetic International Film Festival 2026!
cinema.instructions:
INSTRUCTIONS

Desktop

• Mouse to look around | [Esc] to release

• [W][A][S][D] or [←][↑][↓][→] to move

• Point on a poster to watch Movie

Mobile

• Drag to look around

• Touch the on-screen to move

• Point on a poster to watch Movie

works.title: The Tower Protocol
works.description:
The Tower Protocol: Resurrection – After a mission in 2004 recovering mysterious dual-use technology, Colonel Johnathan White disappeared. Betrayed and alone, he monitors paranormal and occult activities from the shadows. 
Sinister cultists with ancient mystical abilities are gathering, they use Amon-Ra’s ancient powers and the book of the dead; they aim to revive ancient evils and end the world. To succeed, they must eliminate the last Guardian, the retired Tower Group Director, retired Astronaut Steven Church and his top agent COLONEL Johnathan White, sacrificing them both for the chance to resurrect the ruinous powers!
The fate of the world awaits their actions.

works.title: Thy Kingdom Come
works.description: To fight the anxiety that haunts him, a young artist decides to explore the dark side within him. An initiatory quest which will lead him in the footsteps of Satanism in France, from the legends of yesterday to the black masses of today.

works.title: Tony
works.description: After finding a fortune, a man embarks on a hallucinatory quest for truth, as a plea for humanity and reverence for nature resounds.

works.title: Touching Infinity
works.description:
After a crisis of faith and a heartbreak too deep to comprehend, Mary clicks on a 20-minute guided meditation — and falls headfirst into a surreal inner odyssey. What begins as breathwork unravels into a psychedelic spiral of Instagram enlightenment, fake Tantra coaches, religious guilt, and spiritual gaslighting.
A genre-defying blend of dark comedy, spiritual satire, and metaphysical horror, Touching Infinity dives into the messy reality of the spiritual path — a landscape full of real transformation and real predators, sacred teachings and ego traps, insight and illusion.

works.title: UNO (The End Of Evil)
works.description:
Humanity wanders the desolate domain of the Archons, a vast prison of matter where the intellect has long been forced to drink from the bitter waters of oblivion. Across this barren wasteland, lost souls roam among the ruins of time, bearing the heavy hammer of hatred as they pursue the last seekers who still dare to look toward the horizon, where the fading light of Lost Knowledge trembles like a distant star.
Beyond the desert, across the "Sea of the Intelligible", something remains — a fragile radiance, almost forgotten.
The ineffable light of ONE:
 perhaps a memory,
 perhaps a promise,
 or perhaps only a vibrant mirage
 at the End of Evil.
"A film  inspired by the Secret book of John and the meditations of Plotinus"

works.title: Venus Im Pelz
works.description: Music Video for "Die Buben Im Pelz" (Band, Vienna)

works.title: What happened...
works.description: What happened with Nath_666? Natalia, a young woman obsessed with witchcraft, recruits a few friends to perform esoteric experiments for her channel. But what was supposed to be a simple ritual spirals out of control

works.title: Whirlybird
works.description:
Whirlybird takes us on a surreal inner journey back to self.
This experimental short explores our potential re-connection to nature and its cycles.
Travelling through space and time, we meet two of the most powerful archetypes that reside in the collective unconscious - the Horned God and the Triple Goddess, guides that seemingly offer an alternative way of life, a return path to our own intuition, true nature and inner sovereignty.

works.title: Hermetic Film Festival
works.description: We want to celebrate the visions - cinematography and Hermetica are both terrific imaginatively visionary cultures. Contemporary esotericism is intimately, and increasingly, connected with popular culture and new media: for this reason, we want to support such a visual fascination, the storylines revolving around neglected knowledge and the frantic search for the divine upon hidden paths of spirituality.

works.title: Holy Mountain Gallery
works.description: The Holy Mountain Gallery is the official venue partner of the Hermetic International Film Festival. “The Holy” is currently developing an Infinite Virtual Museum and a global Map of Artists. Check it out for exhibitions and more.

works.title: Sacrisoft
works.description: Sacrisoft is the official technical partner of the Hermetic International Film Festival and developed this virtual cinema. The company creates a wide range of innovative independent software, including a Landing Page Creator for your next film project, a Video Art Remix Machine, and an awesome Movie Poster Maker. These tools will be available worldwide soon, so stay tuned.

works.title: Gifigo
works.description: Gifigo is the official media partner of the Hermetic International Film Festival, publishing festival reviews and fresh insights on tech, culture, and society on the go.

works.title: La Xaula - Tarot Game
works.artist: Gerard Gil, Sara Ferro, Chris Weil
works.description: Play the 'La Xaula' Video Game. Interpret the unseen. Reveal the Oracle.

works.title: Secropolis
works.description:
In a secret multiverse shaped by an Artificial Oracle (AO) trapped in an ancient Etruscan quantum computer, Taitle, Master of the Labyrinth, is caught in a lucid dream. Only Vecu, a code-born Nymph, can invoke lost rites to save him before all collapses.
Etruscan culture meets modern sci-fi in a groundbreaking feature, filmed for the first time in the secret city of La Scarzuola and Umbria’s ancient Etruscan Vie Cave. Blending original Etruscan language, quantum physics, and multiverse theory with mythology, the film explores a lost heritage.

works.title: White Gardenia...
works.description: White Gardenia - John Calvin and Other Excerpts From the King James Bible

works.title: Yokoji
works.description: Yokoji is a meditative documentary that explores the daily rhythms and inner world of the residents of Yokoji Zen Mountain Center, a remote Buddhist lay-monastery nestled in Southern California’s San Jacinto Mountains. Shot in the middle of the monastery’s intensive three month retreat period, the film offers a rare and intimate look into the intensity and commitment of communal lay monastic life. Yokoji invites viewers to slow down, turn inward, and encounter the stillness that lies beneath the noise of modern life.
